videos.title: The Briefing: Financial Services
videos.summary: Large banks are deploying Claude across their organizations. Anthropic hosted a virtual event called "The Briefing: Financial Services" where banking leaders discussed their implementations and use cases of Claude.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 with Harness Engineering & Loops Will Do Everything.
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Claude Fable 5 and engineering AI for regulated industries, covering open versus closed source models, risks of model shutdowns, and implications for SaaS. The discussion touches on harness engineering and loops as approaches for building AI applications.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Bedrock enables deployment of Claude Code with data residency compliance, featuring cross-region inference, prompt caching, cost tracking per team, and content guardrails. The new Mantle endpoint provides an additional deployment option for organizations with strict compliance requirements.

videos.title: Building long-running AI agents with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on building long-running AI agents using the open source Agent Development Kit (ADK). The tutorial covers the long-running agent pattern with stateless, event-driven architecture and multi-agent delegation patterns.

videos.title: Better Free AI Image than Midjourney?
videos.summary: Jack Roberts argues that Ideogram 4.0 solves the long-standing problem of text rendering in AI-generated images, outperforming Midjourney and Google's Nano Banana Pro on DesignArena benchmarks while offering a free tier. He positions it as viable for brand asset generation tasks.

videos.title: How Machiavelli's Florence bargained with Cesare Borgia for survival – Ada Palmer
videos.summary: Ada Palmer discusses Machiavelli as a misunderstood historical figure, focusing on his diplomatic career in Florence and how his experiences observing European rulers informed his political philosophy. She frames Machiavelli's decision to compile his insights into The Prince as a patriotic act comparable to Szilard's nuclear warning to FDR.

videos.title: Coding with ADK to beat a text adventure game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech livestream featuring Tilde Thurium and Abdel Sghiouar demonstrating the Agent Development Kit (ADK) to solve a text adventure game. The stream aired on June 17 at 9:30 A.M. PDT.

videos.title: Building with ADK & A2A: How multi-agent collaboration designs a game
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates multi-agent collaboration using the Agent Development Kit and Agent2Agent protocol to design game assets and architecture. The demo showcases distinct AI agents working together with human-in-the-loop oversight on Cloud Run.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a coming inflection point in robotics should prioritize human interaction and delight over utility alone, and that the focus on humanoid robots by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a narrow vision. She discusses how billions of robots will enter society and the importance of intentional design philosophy rather than intelligence alone.

videos.title: Is Unstructured Data The Key To Successful AI Deployments?
videos.summary: A podcast discussion from Hyland CommunityLIVE 2026 featuring Hyland leadership and Erie Insurance executives arguing that unstructured data is essential for successful agentic AI deployments in enterprises. The speakers discuss how proper use of unstructured data context helps AI pilots avoid common pitfalls like token misallocation.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: Ziv Ilan's team at NVIDIA achieved near real-time video diffusion generation on Blackwell B200 by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching, and step distillation to reduce the typical 50-step denoising process to as few as one step. The distillation methods include trajectory-based and distribution-based training approaches, with distribution-based training being more common and higher quality.

videos.title: Gemini Omni Tutorial: How to Use Gemini Omni AI for Beginners
videos.summary: A tutorial demonstrating Google Omni Flash for video generation and editing workflows. The video tests physics simulation, character consistency, scene memory, and real footage editing, comparing Omni to Sora and Runway Gen 2 while identifying current limitations and use cases.

videos.title: Kagenti’s Approach to Multi-Agent Security for AI Agents
videos.summary: Kagenti is a security solution for multi-agent AI systems that uses identity-based controls, delegation chains, and open source infrastructure to prevent data leaks and breaches. It addresses the risk of confused agents leaking data in production agentic AI deployments.

videos.title: This command SAVED my broken AI coding session
videos.summary: A YouTube video from JavaScript Mastery describes a command that resolved issues in an AI-assisted coding workflow. The specific command and its mechanics are detailed in the full video.

videos.title: Tencent Workbuddy: RIP Claude,Codex! This is ACTUALLY quite a bit CRAZY!
videos.summary: Tencent WorkBuddy is a desktop AI productivity agent for office work including file reading, spreadsheet analysis, report generation, and presentation creation. It features 100+ domain expert agents, multi-agent expert teams, remote control via Slack/Telegram/Discord/WeChat, and skill marketplace extensions with pricing starting around $10/month.

videos.title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
videos.summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power edge AI development board featuring the Coral NPU accelerator, designed to run Gemma models on-device. The kit includes peripherals such as a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, with open-source demos showcasing live translation, natural language control, and multimodal AI inference entirely on the board.

videos.title: Google Just Revealed What Comes After AGI And It’s Shocking
videos.summary: Google DeepMind published a paper titled "From AGI to ASI" arguing that AGI may not be an endpoint but the beginning of a competitive race toward artificial superintelligence. The paper suggests that once human-level AI is achieved, copying, accelerating, and networking such systems for further AI development could enable rapid progression to superintelligence, making ASI advancement potentially more consequential than AGI itself.

videos.title: Stop testing your SAAS manually
videos.summary: KaneAI is an automated testing platform for SaaS applications that accepts plain English descriptions of tests, generates and runs them automatically, and reports results. It aims to reduce manual QA effort and testing costs by automating the full testing pipeline.

videos.title: This MCP makes Hermes Agent 10x more powerful
videos.summary: Apify MCP Connectors are integrations that significantly amplify the capabilities of Hermes Agent when used together. The connectors provide structured data access through a universal MCP framework.

videos.title: How to build reliable software with AI agents
videos.summary: A discussion exploring how AI-powered coding agents impact software reliability and technical debt. The speakers advocate for human-in-the-loop verification and rigorous code review processes as essential practices to ensure AI agents reduce maintenance costs and keep codebases stable during accelerated development.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Banned... Do THIS Right Now
videos.summary: Jack Roberts discusses the removal of Claude Fable 5 and argues that users should run language models locally using Ollama to maintain access to capable models independently of cloud service providers. He demonstrates how to set up local inference and build an open-source NotebookLM alternative, along with a model routing system across multiple providers.

videos.title: SpaceX’s IPO Triumph, Anthropic’s Fable Fumble, OpenAI’s Price War
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss SpaceX's IPO, its implications for AI competitors OpenAI and Anthropic, Anthropic's Fable rollout issues, and competitive pricing dynamics in the AI model market. They cover whether AI models are becoming commoditized and Apple's Siri improvements.

videos.title: The magic behind GenLatte: Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini
videos.summary: GenLatte is a generative UI application demonstrated at Google Cloud Next '26 that combines Flutter, Firebase, and Gemini to generate personalized latte art. The app uses agentic features and Agent-to-UI (A2UI) to dynamically create custom images on demand based on individual customer preferences.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that enables developers to orchestrate multiple AI coding models such as Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers. It allows routing tasks to different models for specific jobs like implementation and code review, includes human-in-the-loop governance, and maintains sessions across devices.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe architecture isolates MCP and ChatGPT apps by nesting an srcdoc iframe inside a controlled-subdomain iframe. This pattern prevents Content Security Policy bypasses, credential theft, and sandbox escapes by separating app code (inner frame) from script loading (outer frame). Each app gets a unique subdomain to prevent cross-app storage collisions.

videos.title: What Is AI Pair Programming? AI Coding Tools for Developers
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents an overview of AI pair programming and its applications in software development. The video covers how AI tools assist developers in debugging, code review, and improving productivity through AI-assisted coding practices.

videos.title: FULLY FREE Unlimited API + OpenCode: MiniMax M3,Step 3.7 Flash,Nemotron 3 Ultra,GLM,Kimi!
videos.summary: OpenRouter introduced Fusion API, a compound model system that sends prompts to multiple models and uses a judge model to select the final answer. The creator tested it against claimed Fable-level performance and found inconsistent results across real-world tasks, noting it can be slower and more expensive than using strong single models.

videos.title: Why I never trust fully generated Code
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery author expresses distrust in fully generated code from AI systems. The piece represents a viewpoint on code generation reliability and best practices for developers.

videos.title: The Internet Is No Longer Human
videos.summary: Bots now exceed human web traffic; Google AI Overviews reduce click-through rates; AI agents increasingly act on behalf of users for browsing and commerce. The video argues this represents a fundamental handoff of internet control from humans to machines.

videos.title: STOP Using Claude Code Without This Fable 5 Agentic OS
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates how to set up Fable 5 Agentic OS with Claude Code, covering architecture, memory, observability, and customizable skills to optimize agentic workflows. The tutorial shows practical implementation steps for building agency automation systems.

videos.title: You Don't Need React Portals For Modals Anymore (react-call)
videos.summary: react-call is a lightweight React utility that enables calling components as asynchronous functions, replacing the need for React Portals when building modals. It uses Root and Stack, Upsert Singletons, and Mutation Flow patterns to handle layout isolation and state management without context providers or complex state tracking.

videos.title: Type This Into Claude, It'll Make You Build 10x Faster.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese presents six specific phrases to type into Claude Code that allegedly accelerate project development by 10x. The approach emphasizes particular prompting patterns endorsed by Claude Code's creator and Andrej Karpathy, alongside a cautionary anti-pattern that he claims most users misuse.

videos.title: Smart AI routing explained
videos.summary: IBM Technology reports that AI labs are prioritizing intelligent routing mechanisms over raw model size. Cost efficiency and trustworthiness are now central concerns driving this strategic shift.

videos.title: How to ACTUALLY Use AI For Coding
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates a new Codex feature called /goal that allows developers to describe desired outcomes rather than step-by-step instructions. The system enters a "Ralph Loop" that autonomously plans steps, executes them, tests outputs, and self-corrects without user intervention during execution.

videos.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
videos.summary: Will Scott explains how design systems and context engineering guide intelligent agents in software design. The discussion covers how MCP and AI agents work together to build consistent, correct applications with structured design guidance.

videos.title: Build a Hermes Knowledge Base That Self-Improves
videos.summary: Build a self-improving knowledge base for Hermes AI agent using an Obsidian wiki connected to Claude and NotebookLM, with automatic ingestion of meetings, emails, and research via Granola and cron jobs. The approach applies Andrej Karpathy's LLM wiki principles to give AI agents persistent, growing memory across documents.

videos.title: OpenRouter Fusion: They OFFICIALLY CLAIM THAT THIS MODEL beats FABLE?
videos.summary: AICodeKing reviews OpenRouter's Fusion API, which uses parallel model calls and a judge model to claim Fable-level performance at half the cost. Testing across simulators, SVG generation, math, and coding reveals inconsistent results worse than single strong models, suggesting marketing claims are misleading and the system is overhyped.

videos.title: RIP Claude Fable, open-source AI unleashed, full body avatars, new Google models, new TTS: AI NEWS
videos.summary: A YouTube video news roundup covering recent AI developments including new model releases (GLM 5.2, Kimi K2.7, MiniMax M3), avatar and video generation tools, text-to-speech advances, and discussion of Claude Fable's discontinuation. The video reviews multiple open-source and commercial AI tools and research projects.

videos.title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
videos.summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model capable of exceeding 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model employs selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed and eliminate bottlenecks.

videos.title: Fable 5 is never coming back, here's why
videos.summary: David Ondrej claims Fable 5 was banned and frames this as the beginning of a permanent technological underclass. The video title suggests the product will not make a comeback.

videos.title: This Is Bad... They Just Shut Down FABLE 5
videos.summary: Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models were shut down by US government order after only three days of public access. The video discusses implications for frontier AI regulation and public access to powerful models, questioning whether governments will restrict the strongest AI systems behind regulatory walls.

videos.title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
videos.summary: The video argues that GPT-6's delayed release has allowed Microsoft, Apple, and Google to move away from OpenAI's models, with Microsoft integrating Project Polaris into GitHub Copilot, Apple adopting Gemini, and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 reportedly achieving higher benchmark scores than GPT-5.5. The piece frames this as a loss of OpenAI's market dominance amid broader industry shifts.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: A YouTuber discusses the withdrawal of Claude Fable 5 after a three-day public launch, citing US government export-control directives and alleged jailbreak concerns. The video covers the regulatory event, model performance benchmarks relative to Claude Opus, and implications for developers building on these models.

videos.title: 1,000+ Tokens/Sec: Google Just Shattered the AI Speed Limit (DiffusionGemma)
videos.summary: DiffusionGemma is Google DeepMind's text generation model that applies diffusion-based image generation techniques to language modeling using uniform state diffusion. The architecture achieves over 1,000 tokens per second on H100 GPUs by shifting from memory-bound to compute-bound operations through bidirectional multi-pass token refinement.

videos.title: This AI Chinese model is 15X cheaper?
videos.summary: MiniMax M3 is a long-context AI model released on OpenRouter that uses Sparse Attention to process long documents 15.6x faster and at one-twentieth the compute cost of its predecessor. The model selectively focuses on relevant portions of input rather than processing entire documents.

videos.title: GLM-5.2 (Fully Tested): I got EARLY ACCESS & This MODEL is CRAZY!
videos.summary: GLM-5.2 is an improved version of GLM-5.1 from Zhipu AI supporting a 1 million token context window and expected to be released as open-weight with MIT licensing. The model achieves strong coding performance benchmarks (81.43 score) and is offered through the GLM Coding Plan at $8 per month, competing with Claude and Codex on cost and speed.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: BREAKING: US Government BANNED Mythos and Fable
videos.summary: The US government issued an export control directive forcing Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models for all customers immediately. The announcement cites regulatory compliance as the basis for the discontinuation.

videos.title: Google’s New AI Just Broke The AI Speed Limit: DiffusionGemma
videos.summary: Google released DiffusionGemma, an open AI model that generates text using a different approach from standard autoregressive methods and achieves up to four times faster text generation on dedicated GPUs. The model features a 256-token canvas and self-correction capabilities.

videos.title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
videos.summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform that enables developers to build, orchestrate, and monitor AI agents. It offers multiple interfaces including a desktop app (2.0), IDE, CLI, and SDK to support different agentic workflows and connect agents to code editors, terminals, and browsers.

videos.title: AIventure: Vibe Coding Journey
videos.summary: AIventure is an open source retro dungeon crawler built on Angular and Phaser.js that teaches vibe coding and agentic behaviors using Gemma 4. It demonstrates NPC prompt engineering for code generation and autonomous thinking loops with tool calls.

videos.title: PI AGENT FULL COURSE: Master Pi Agent in 30 Minutes
videos.summary: David Ondrej presents a complete course on Pi Agent, a framework for building real coding agents. The course covers mastering Pi Agent fundamentals in a structured format, with emphasis on practical AI coding skills and agent development capabilities.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Just Changed Website Design Forever
videos.summary: Jack Roberts compares Claude Fable 5 (Opus 4.8) against another model in progressive website design tasks, testing one-shot generation, hard mode challenges, and design extraction workflows. The video argues Fable 5 delivers superior results through strategic token usage and attention to design details.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is smarter💡
videos.summary: IBM Technology presents an overview of Claude Fable 5, highlighting improvements in planning capabilities, code analysis depth, and inference speed. The episode is part of the Mixture of Experts series.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music streaming service made and curated by musicians, designed to provide ambient music for thinking and building. It is produced by Anthropic's Claude team and available on YouTube.

videos.title: Claude FM 🎵 music for thinking and building
videos.summary: Claude FM is a music streaming service curated by musicians and designed to accompany focused work and thinking. It is made and offered by Anthropic as a companion product to Claude.

videos.title: How to Use Higgsfield AI Like a PRO – AI Marketing Workflow
videos.summary: Higgsfield Supercomputer is an AI super-agent that generates full marketing campaigns from a single prompt, including TV ads, podcasts, reels, and character-driven shorts. The platform automates multi-format content creation while maintaining visual consistency across outputs.

videos.title: Converting SQL Server code to PostgreSQL
videos.summary: Google Cloud demonstrates using Gemini AI as a tutor for converting SQL Server T-SQL code to PostgreSQL. Gemini explains data type mappings and PostgreSQL concepts, helping developers migrate schemas and apps to cloud-based PostgreSQL environments.

videos.title: The Rise of Generative UI for Developers (CopilotKit)
videos.summary: CopilotKit is a framework enabling developers to move beyond basic chatbot windows by allowing AI agents to render native UI components, share application state with React and Next.js apps, and implement human-in-the-loop approval workflows. The video explores how generative UI (AG-UI) integrates agentic behavior directly into applications compared to alternatives like Vercel AI SDK.

videos.title: How I make my AI Agent remember everything
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery covers techniques for enabling AI agents to retain and recall information across interactions. The content demonstrates practical implementation approaches for agent memory systems.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how to compete against incumbent giants in enterprise software. She describes how even large innovative companies like Google rely on spreadsheets for strategic planning, making the case for purpose-built AI performance management platforms that consolidate finance, supply chain, HR, and sales data.

videos.title: Don’t miss this Claude update
videos.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables the model to launch hundreds of subagents simultaneously within a single session. The system automatically plans work, splits tasks, and coordinates parallel execution with built-in verification, designed for large codebases, cross-file research, and complex multi-step tasks.

videos.title: The $20,000 API Key Mistake
videos.summary: Keith AI presents a case study of a $20,000 incident involving improper API key handling. The video serves as a warning about the financial risks of security mismanagement in AI API usage.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 & Apple’s NVIDIA deal
videos.summary: A Mixture of Experts podcast episode hosted by Tim Hwang examining Claude Fable 5's tier routing system, Apple's shift away from on-device AI toward NVIDIA cloud compute partnership announced at WWDC 2026, and debating whether AI models can detect sarcasm using multimodal inputs or context alone.

videos.title: The “Final Boss” of AI Workflows: UltraCode
videos.summary: Keith AI discusses UltraCode as a solution for AI workflows, framing it as an advanced or comprehensive approach to workflow management. The video title suggests UltraCode represents a significant capability in the AI workflow space.

videos.title: Claude Code (Fable) V/S Codex (GPT-5.5) : MATHEMATICAL TEST = ONE CLEAR WINNER!
videos.summary: The video compares subscription plans like Claude Pro, Claude Max, ChatGPT Plus, and ChatGPT Pro against API pricing. It estimates that Claude Pro at $20/month offers around $400 in API-equivalent value while the $200 ChatGPT Pro plan may provide up to $14,000 in usage value if fully utilized.

videos.title: Master Claude Code with Claude Fable 5 (57 mins)
videos.summary: Keith AI's 57-minute video demonstrates advanced use of Claude Code as a coding system rather than a chat interface. The video covers Desktop, CLI, and web modes, permissions, connectors, slash commands, plugins, routines, sub-agents, and dynamic workflows for building apps and automating development workflows.

videos.title: Code with Claude Tokyo 2026: Opening Keynote
videos.summary: Anthropic hosted an opening keynote at Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, featuring updates from engineering and product leaders. The event showcases the latest developments from the company.

videos.title: The Fable 5 Backlash Is Getting Serious
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is facing backlash over overly aggressive safety filters that refuse harmless prompts, potentially fall back to weaker models, and apply invisible guardrails. The controversy centers on user trust: whether closed AI systems can be relied upon when their true capabilities and constraints are hidden.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Yossi Matias on the golden age of research
videos.summary: Yossi Matias, VP and Head of Google Research, discusses Google's "magic cycle" connecting research breakthroughs to real-world impact. The conversation covers AI tools for scientific discovery, speculative decoding, generative UI, and quantum computing achievements including the Willow chip.

videos.title: Your first agent configuration with MCP and Looker
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech video on building AI-powered agents using Agent Development Kit (ADK) and Looker. Covers Model Context Protocol (MCP), the MCP Toolbox for Databases, tools.yaml configuration, Looker API setup, and creating functional agents that translate prompts into data requests.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 + YouTube = $30,000/mo
videos.summary: A YouTube content production pipeline using Claude Code Fable 5 to automatically deconstruct successful channels, generate scripts, and edit videos with AI-generated visuals. The system combines reverse engineering of channels like Fireship, voice recording, and Higsfield CLI for automated editing to produce faceless YouTube content at roughly $2 per video.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace where non-engineers can ask business questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language. The LLM generates reusable JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap. Reliability comes from preflight sequencing to manage context, explicit distrust rules for hallucinations, and query validation before widget creation.

videos.title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
videos.summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses with Baseten representatives the direction of scaling coding agents beyond current limits. The conversation covers running agents at scale (128+ agents), enabling inter-agent communication and review mechanisms, and shifting from task parallelism to building cohesive multi-agent systems.

videos.title: Meta' Secret Plan To Kill The Smartphone — With Alex Himel
videos.summary: Alex Himel, Meta's VP of Wearables, discusses how AI-powered smart glasses could replace smartphones as the next major computing platform. The conversation covers potential applications like task assistance, meetings, and real-world context awareness, as well as competitive dynamics with OpenAI, Google, Apple, and Amazon, and privacy concerns around on-device facial recognition and AI.

videos.title: Local AI Agents Masterclass (Gemma 4 + OpenCode + Odysseus tutorial)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates how to run two types of AI agents locally: Odysseus (a workspace agent) for task orchestration and OpenCode (a build agent) for code generation. The setup uses Gemma 4 via Ollama for local inference with Venice API as fallback, deployed in Docker for privacy-focused local execution.

videos.title: 21 Hermes Lessons to Get You Started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents a foundational tutorial covering 21 key lessons for getting started with Hermes, an open-source language model framework. The content is designed to introduce developers to core concepts and practical usage patterns.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that simplifies agent interaction with websites by replacing DOM scraping with structured tools. It provides both declarative HTML-based and imperative JavaScript-based APIs for defining typed, named actions that agents can call directly, reducing the need for visual parsing and coordinate math.

videos.title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
videos.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard protocol that enables AI agents to communicate with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a client-server architecture using JSON-RPC. MCP supports both local and remote server connections via STDIO and HTTP, and is becoming essential for multi-agent AI systems built with frameworks like LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser describes a sustainable AI agent workflow for developers where human attention is the bottleneck, not agent speed. The system uses signal agents to manage notifications, verification gates for safety, conversation history analysis for self-improvement, and integrated health monitoring via Oura ring to support developer wellbeing.

videos.title: Gemini Omni's Best Feature
videos.summary: Google's Gemini Omni can add objects to existing video footage by accepting text, image, or audio descriptions and automatically handling lighting and shading. It enables real-time post-production-style object insertion into existing clips.

videos.title: "An endless demand for compute" | Jonathan Ross, founder of Groq
videos.summary: Groq is a hardware chip specifically designed for LLM inference. Jonathan Ross, the founder, discusses Groq's LPU architecture, its comparison to GPUs via Pareto curves and cost-per-token metrics, and a strategic agreement with NVIDIA. The interview covers technical aspects like static scheduling and mixture-of-experts models.

videos.title: AI That’s Too Dangerous For You? What we learned from S.A.T.A.N
videos.summary: IBM discusses how AI is detecting zero-day vulnerabilities faster than before and its impact on cybersecurity risk management. The video explains AI-driven vulnerability scanning and DevSecOps as mechanisms to improve system safety.

videos.title: The AI Skill I use to prevent refactors
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses an AI-based skill or technique for preventing code refactors. The content suggests practical application of AI in software development workflows to improve code quality and maintainability.

videos.title: THE $1 CODER: This CODER COSTS $1 AND GIVES YOU $50 WORTH USAGE!
videos.summary: Command Code is a coding agent harness optimized for open-source models like DeepSeek, Qwen, and others. It improves agent performance through routing, caching, context handling, and tool-call repair. The platform offers affordable pricing starting at one dollar per month with evaluation claims that DeepSeek V4 Pro outperforms Opus 4.7 on hard tool-heavy tasks.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The architecture combines hybrid mamba transformer, MTP (Multi-head Token Pooling), and Latent MoE for improved performance.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
videos.summary: AI Search published a YouTube review of Claude Fable 5 (also called Claude Mythos 5), comparing it against GPT 5.5 across multiple benchmarks and capability demonstrations. The review covers ray tracing, 3D reconstruction, music composition, and chemistry capabilities.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that simplifies building and deploying AI agents to production. It provides features including agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade deployments.

videos.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
videos.summary: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video editing app in one hour by feeding Claude Fable 5 only screenshots of a SaaS landing page via Claude Code. The model successfully generated functional code including filler removal, clip detection, and subtitle export, though Fable 5 consumes API limits 2x faster than Opus and will be discontinued on June 22.

videos.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech demonstrates building a no-code calendar app in Google AI Studio that extracts travel itineraries and converts them into calendar invitations using Gemini's advanced reasoning capabilities. The tutorial shows how to customize the app to fit specific needs.

videos.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
videos.summary: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 with Claude, recommending an alternative approach instead. The video promotes learning resources and his company Scale Software.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford demonstrates that a 4B model finetuned with RL to enforce tool discipline (calling get_table_name, inspecting schemas, self-correcting errors) outperforms the 235B Qwen 3 on query tasks and generalizes from single to multi-table problems. The approach uses rubric-based evaluation to target specific behaviors before data labeling.

videos.title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
videos.summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and builders ship code through agents. Founded by Michael Truell, it has grown from 15 people to 700 employees and serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies, partnering closely with Anthropic.

videos.title: Stop applying AI to the old ways of working!
videos.summary: Andrew Macvean from Google for Developers argues that organizations should not simply apply AI to existing workflows, as coding was never the real bottleneck. The position emphasizes rethinking entire development lifecycles and processes to be designed with and for AI from the ground up.

videos.title: New AI attack reality💥
videos.summary: University of Toronto researchers demonstrated an AI worm, illustrating new attack vectors in cybersecurity. The commentary suggests that stopping AI threats is infeasible and advocates for adaptive defense strategies rather than prevention-focused approaches.

videos.title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
videos.summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that gives AI agents persistent memory of project architecture, coding standards, decisions, and workflows across sessions. It includes a Pulse dashboard and seven-phase algorithm for observe-think-plan-build-execute-verify-learn cycles.

videos.title: Sovereign Escape Velocity: Ownership w Open Models — Gus Martins, & Ian Ballantyne, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemma 4's 31B model achieves fourth place on LM Arena despite being 2-20x smaller than comparable competitors and runs on a single GPU. The model enables efficient deployment on phones, medical systems on two GPUs, and multi-agent workloads on consumer hardware like M4 Macs.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Design – Full AI Design Workflow 2026
videos.summary: Claude Design is an AI design tool from Anthropic that automates design workflows including design system building, landing page generation, and asset creation. The tutorial demonstrates a four-step framework: build design system, generate designs, apply edits, and deploy as templates.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 (TESTED): UHM... It's actually not worth it..
videos.summary: AICodeKing reviews Anthropic's Claude Fable 5, a safeguarded version of Claude Mythos 5. While the model achieves strong results on coding and reasoning benchmarks, the reviewer finds it does not provide a meaningful improvement over existing models like Opus for daily coding work, and notes that pricing and safeguards may limit practical value.

videos.title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
videos.summary: PostHog is developing an autonomous agent pipeline that converts product signals (rage clicks, error spikes, customer messages) into pull requests. The system groups related errors and session replays using LLM-generated query embeddings, researches the codebase, and opens automated fixes.

videos.title: AI agents explained: Build your first agent in 8 minutes
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial demonstrating how to build autonomous, self-correcting multi-agent systems using the Google Agent Development Kit. The content covers the ReAct Framework, three main agent patterns (sequential, reactive, planning), and a practical implementation of a blog writing agent with validation and auto-correction capabilities.

videos.title: The One Mistake Everyone Makes with AI
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery published a video discussing a widespread mistake that people make when working with AI. The video is framed as educational content for developers integrating AI into applications.

videos.title: Can you social engineer an AI? Plus: AI worms and the nonhuman identity problem
videos.summary: IBM Security Intelligence discusses social engineering attacks against AI systems, including prompt injection at Meta/Instagram and self-replicating AI worms. The episode examines whether AI agents are more susceptible to manipulation than humans and highlights rising security risks from unaudited nonhuman identities in enterprise environments.

videos.title: Well, that’s a hot take on light mode for sure!
videos.summary: Google for Developers discusses the tradeoff of using light mode for UI design. The video argues that while light mode keeps users awake, there are costs to consider in the design choice.

videos.title: Anthropic Just Dropped Fable 5 And It’s Terrifying
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, a Mythos-class AI model with capabilities in software engineering, knowledge work, vision, analytics, scientific research, and cybersecurity. The model includes built-in safety systems that restrict its answers in high-risk domains like cybersecurity, biology, and chemistry.

videos.title: $10,000 AI Website in 28mins
videos.summary: Jack Roberts demonstrates building a website comparable to a $10,000 professional project in just 28 minutes using AI tools. The video showcases rapid AI-assisted web development and the efficiency gains from automation.

videos.title: From prompt to a fully working real-time voice app in seconds. 🎙️✨
videos.summary: Google AI Studio enables developers to build, deploy, and remix real-time AI voice applications from a single voice prompt. The tool transforms development from static code to dynamic agentic workflows, reducing deployment time significantly.

videos.title: Apple’s New Siri AI Is Now Gemini
videos.summary: Apple revealed a rebuilt Siri AI at WWDC 2026 with personal context awareness, screen awareness, Visual Intelligence, writing tools, and cross-device conversations integrated across iPhone, iPad, Mac, Apple Watch, and Vision Pro. The new Siri reportedly uses Google Gemini, but regulatory delays in the EU and a cold investor reaction have raised questions about Apple's independent AI capabilities.

videos.title: Claude Mythos is FINALLY here (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos class model, which achieves top performance on benchmarks including SWE Bench Pro and Frontier Code. The model demonstrates capabilities in long-context tasks, vision, and code generation across fullstack and CAD applications.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 beats Pokémon FireRed only using vision
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 successfully completed Pokémon FireRed using only vision-based input from raw game screenshots without maps or navigation aids. This demonstrates improved capabilities compared to earlier Claude models which required a complex helper harness to play the same game.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for tech job hunting in 2026 amid AI-driven resume screening, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and open source contribution over mass applications. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable and that job roles evolve rather than disappear, citing transportation industry analogies.

videos.title: 21 Hermes concepts to get you started
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents 21 foundational concepts related to Hermes, a model or framework. The video serves as an introductory guide for learners new to the system.

videos.title: Multiple claude agent in the same window?
videos.summary: Claude Opus 4.8 introduces Dynamic Workflows, which enables launching hundreds of subagents concurrently within a single session. The system automatically plans work distribution, assigns parallel tasks, and performs verification through dedicated quality assurance agents.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

videos.title: Claude just dropped UltraCode... Unlock SuperPowers
videos.summary: Claude released UltraCode, a feature that enables code-based orchestration of up to 10 parallel agents without a master LLM. The video tutorial covers implementation patterns including judge panels, adversarial verification, deterministic fan-out, and integration with OpenRouter for dynamic model selection.

videos.title: Build an app from your phone in AI Studio
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial demonstrates building a custom image-generation app from a mobile browser using Google AI Studio with voice commands and Nano Banana API. The tool uses AI reasoning to generate code in real time without requiring software installation.

videos.title: Behind the Scenes - Introduction to Artificial Intelligence with Brian Yu - Chapter 1 - Playing
videos.summary: CS50 is Harvard University's introduction to computer science and programming, available through edX, Harvard Extension School, Harvard Summer School, and OpenCourseWare. The course is led by David J. Malan and offers supplementary content including a YouTube channel and active community forums across multiple platforms.

videos.title: GPU Cloud Deployment Without Leaving Your IDE — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: Flash is a deployment tool that simplifies GPU cloud workflows by replacing multi-step Docker and registry processes with a single decorator on async Python functions. It supports hot reload for model swaps and integrates directly with IDEs, with pricing available through RunPod's serverless infrastructure at $0.00116 per second for H100 GPUs.

videos.title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
videos.summary: Military historian Sarah Paine argues that global power for two millennia has divided between continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest (China, Russia) and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade (Britain, US). The framework explains how geography shapes the strategies of leaders like Putin and Xi.

videos.title: Claude Mythos 5 + Fable 5 Are Here And The Numbers Are INSANE
videos.summary: Anthropic released Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5 (Mythos 5 with guardrails). Fable 5 is available at no extra cost on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22, after which it requires usage credits. The release emphasizes performance improvements with impressive benchmark numbers.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 plays Factorio
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 autonomously plays Factorio, a factory-building game, strategizing and constructing automated systems independently. This demonstrates the model's reasoning and planning capabilities applied to a complex optimization domain.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 simulates the solar system and predicts a solar eclipse
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 simulated the solar system by deriving planetary orbital motion from physics first principles. The model used this simulation to predict solar eclipses.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 sets a fluid simulation to Beethoven
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 generated code for a fluid simulation synchronized to a classical-EDM remix. The system also produced the music itself using code, despite having no prior exposure to audio.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 designs a 3D-printable model in a Claude-built CAD editor
videos.summary: Claude Fable 5 is a browser-based CAD editor built by Anthropic that can design 3D-printable models. The editor includes a built-in AI copilot that performs the modeling tasks automatically.

videos.title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
videos.summary: Kuba Rogut contends that RAG is not dead but redefined as agentic search combining vector search, full text search, regex, and filters iteratively. Cursor's indexed semantic search achieved 24% answer accuracy gains while Claude Code's grep-based approach trades upfront indexing cost for runtime simplicity, reflecting a tradeoff on caching compute based on query volume.

videos.title: GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening with cuML and Agent Platform
videos.summary: Google Cloud and NVIDIA demonstrate GPU-accelerated virtual drug screening using cuML and Agent Platform, achieving 20x-45x training speedups on tabular molecular data without rewriting existing pandas or scikit-learn code. The approach enables billion-molecule scale predictions for drug discovery applications like EGFR lung cancer binding prediction.

videos.title: From Transcription to Live Music: Gemini's Audio Stack — Thor Schaeff, Google DeepMind
videos.summary: Gemini 3 Flash Preview provides audio understanding through a single API call, extracting speaker labels, timestamps, emotion tags, language detection with translation, and summaries. It serves as the foundation for higher-level audio processing in Google DeepMind's audio stack.

videos.title: Stop Prompting Claude. Use Karpathy's Method Instead.
videos.summary: Austin Marchese argues that most users prompt Claude ineffectively and recommends a three-layer method attributed to Andrej Karpathy, former AI head at Tesla, for faster development with Claude and Claude Code. The approach contrasts with conventional prompting techniques and emphasizes building 10x faster using this structured methodology.

videos.title: How To Design a Complete Brand Identity With AI (Best AI Logo Maker)
videos.summary: Design.com is an AI-powered platform for rapid brand identity creation, enabling users to generate logos, brand kits, business cards, and social media assets from plain-English descriptions. The platform applies consistent branding across 500,000+ templates and includes features like AI background removal, presentation generation, and animated logo creation.

videos.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Live Translate
videos.summary: Google DeepMind introduced Gemini 3.5 Live Translate, a speech-to-speech translation model enabling real-time multilingual translation across 70+ languages. The model supports live video dubbing, automatic language switching, and natural-sounding output, available to developers via the Gemini API and in Google Translate, AI Studio, and Google Meet.

videos.title: Kimi Code is good at video reasoning with Kimi K2.6 🎬
videos.summary: Kimi Code is a video reasoning tool built on Kimi K2.6 that accepts reference videos and generates .cube LUT (color lookup table) files based on visual queries about colors, shots, or visual style. Users can drag in video references and request specific visual attributes to generate ready-to-use color grading outputs.

videos.title: How I'd Learn AI From Scratch in 2026 (skip the useless 80%)
videos.summary: Jeff Su presents a prioritized learning strategy for AI in 2026 centered on picking one model to master, building practical context through real projects, and assembling AI systems with tools. The guidance dismisses rote prompting frameworks in favor of deeper model understanding and contextual work.

videos.title: Finally… A NotebookLM Alternative With an API
videos.summary: Open Notebook is a self-hosted, open-source alternative to Google NotebookLM designed for developers who want control over their docs, code, and AI workflows. It provides an API and supports features like document grounding, question answering, and podcast generation from source materials.

videos.title: This is why my AI Agents never guess
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery discusses why their AI agents are designed to avoid guessing or uncertainty in their responses. The video appears to promote a principle of deterministic or reliable AI agent behavior.

videos.title: Building AI Agent Systems and Scaling Challenges in Agentic AI
videos.summary: Sam Anthony discusses why scaling AI agent systems increases cost, latency, and failure risk. Multi-agent design, coordination, and system architecture are identified as key factors affecting real-world scalability of agentic AI.

videos.title: Code with Claude 2026 | Tokyo
videos.summary: Anthropic held Code with Claude 2026 in Tokyo, a conference featuring announcements about Claude models, the Claude Platform, and Claude Code, with demonstrations from partner organizations Canva, Mizuho, and NRI.

videos.title: Frontier Code (GPT-5.6 VS Mythos): This BENCHMARK is ACTUALLY REAL!
videos.summary: Cognition released FrontierCode, a benchmark that measures whether AI-generated code is mergeable by real maintainers, using blocker criteria and weighted scores rather than just test passage. It covers tasks from 36 open-source repositories and compares models like Claude Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5 on code quality standards beyond unit tests.

videos.title: Gemma Playground: Robot Duck
videos.summary: Xavier Plantaz demonstrates running Gemma 4 E2B on Raspberry Pi 5 and Jetson Orin Nano using LiteRT, with on-device multimodal voice interface built from Parakeet, Gemma 4, and Kokoro, applied to Open Duck Mini v2 robots for real-time conversation and sensory feedback.

videos.title: New BEST local AI image generator is here!
videos.summary: Ideogram 4 is an open-source local AI image generator featuring text rendering, prompt adherence, bounding box control, and support for comics and manga generation. The video demonstrates integration with ComfyUI workflow and installation of supporting nodes.

videos.title: 2026 AI Engineer Vibe Reel
videos.summary: AI Engineer channel is promoting the World's Fair in San Francisco scheduled for June 29 to July 2, 2026. Tickets and schedule information are available at ai.engineer/wf.

videos.title: Graphify + Obsidian + Claude Code = CHEAT CODE
videos.summary: Chase AI demonstrates a workflow combining Graphify for repository analysis, Obsidian for knowledge organization, and Claude Code for code generation. The approach merges knowledge graph insights into Obsidian vaults to improve Claude Code's contextual outputs.

videos.title: ChatGPT Is Getting Its Biggest Upgrade Ever
videos.summary: OpenAI is reportedly transforming ChatGPT from a simple chatbot into a full AI super app integrating Codex, agents, image tools, third-party apps, and workplace automation. The upgrade includes Lockdown Mode for security against prompt injection attacks and represents a shift toward positioning ChatGPT as the central AI layer for work and daily tasks.

videos.title: Working Like a Lawyer with Claude
videos.summary: Mark Pike from Anthropic and Anna Gressel from Freshfields discuss how lawyers use Claude for legal work. They explore how AI is transforming legal practice while emphasizing that judgment calls remain with the lawyer.

videos.title: Stop managing infrastructure for AI Agents 🛠️
videos.summary: Google announced managed Agents in the Gemini API that handle infrastructure automatically. Users call a single API endpoint to deploy autonomous agents in a Google-hosted sandbox without managing servers.

videos.title: Intro to multi-agent systems with ADK
videos.summary: Google Cloud Tech tutorial on the Agent Development Kit, a framework for building and testing multi-agent systems with custom tools and debugging interfaces. The tutorial covers creating agents, integrating real-time data retrieval and search capabilities, and reducing hallucinations through system design.

videos.title: Claude Code + Graphify = Insane Agentic OS
videos.summary: Graphify builds a knowledge graph representation of code projects, allowing Claude to understand repository structure without re-reading entire codebases. This reduces token costs, latency, and improves accuracy when working with Claude Code for agentic systems.

videos.title: Road to 5 Million Tokens: Breaking Barriers in Long Context Training — Max Ryabinin, Together AI
videos.summary: Max Ryabinin from Together AI presents techniques for training LLMs with extremely long context windows up to 5 million tokens on limited hardware. The work combines fully sharded data parallelism, DeepSpeed Ulysses context parallelism, activation checkpointing, CPU offloading, and a novel method called Untied Ulysses that further optimizes attention head buffer reuse.

videos.title: Reflecting on a year of Claude Code
videos.summary: Claude Code is an agentic coding tool that runs in the terminal and has been generally available for one year. The video features Claude Code leaders discussing verification practices, auto mode, adoption patterns, and emerging features like loops and agent orchestration across organizational engineering teams.

videos.title: AI YouTube Automation: How to Start a YouTube Channel With AI
videos.summary: Arthur walks through a complete AI-driven YouTube video production pipeline from idea research to publishing, covering research, scripting, voice cloning, avatar generation, editing, and repurposing. The workflow integrates ChatGPT, Claude, HeyGen, Descript, Opus Clip, and other tools into an automated system that produces a finished video in 20 minutes.

videos.title: Simulating Percolation in Python: How Do Wildfires & Diseases Spread?
videos.summary: This video tutorial demonstrates how to implement percolation simulations in Python to model the spread of wildfires and diseases. Percolation theory is used to understand how phenomena propagate through networks and systems.

videos.title: Did Apple (Finally) Get AI Right At WWDC?, Anthropic’s Worry, Microsoft vs. OpenAI
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and Ranjan Roy discuss whether Apple's AI strategy at WWDC addresses genuine capability or market perception. The discussion covers Anthropic's claims about AI self-improvement, tensions between Microsoft and OpenAI, and concerns about AI safety pressures in public companies.

videos.title: Inside Google Antigravity 2.0: The complete developer guide | The Agent Factory
videos.summary: Google Antigravity 2.0 is an agentic developer platform that unbundles the IDE into a desktop agent manager, CLI, SDK, and custom IDE. It enables orchestration of parallel sub-agents for tasks like code generation, DevOps, and QA, supporting voice prompts and skill-based context compression for maintaining code quality at scale.

videos.title: Hermes Agent is crazy… 180,000+ github stars
videos.summary: Hermes Agent is an agent framework with over 180,000 GitHub stars. The video demonstrates running it with MiniMax's m3 model to reduce costs by approximately 100x compared to alternatives.

videos.title: How A Beauty Company Built An AI Agent — With Rachel Williamson and Josh Siebert
videos.summary: Ulta Beauty deployed ServiceNow's AI-powered HR platform across its 65,000-person workforce to automate HR processes. The implementation included decisions about task automation versus human oversight, personalization over static documentation, and integration with hiring and employee lifecycle workflows.

videos.title: Why More Context Makes Your Agent Dumber and What to Do About It — Nupur Sharma, Qodo
videos.summary: Nupur Sharma describes the U curve problem where agents attending to context window extremes miss the middle content, then presents iterative retrieval, hierarchical summarization, and self correction as solutions. She also introduces an 80/20 orchestration pattern using high-reasoning models for discovery and lighter deterministic models for validation, applied in Qodo's code review agent architecture with adaptive weighting.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, discusses how to distinguish real product-market fit from false signals. He argues that passionate customers and formal interviews are unreliable, and that true PMF manifests as customer pull rather than founder push. He emphasizes embedded customer relationships and betting on the direction of AI models rather than their current state.

videos.title: Why Eval++ Is the Next Great Compute Primitive — Sunil Pai & Matt Carey, Cloudflare
videos.summary: Cloudflare's Eval++ is a compute primitive that enables sandboxed execution of LLM-generated code with explicit capability grants. Built on Durable Objects and Dynamic Workers, it provides addressable, persistent, stateful compute with low latency suitable for AI agents.

videos.title: Can you spot which committed file explains this mismatch?
videos.summary: A package management puzzle demonstrates how local development and CI environments can install the same declared dependency but end up with different versions despite package.json remaining unchanged. The technique involves analyzing how the install resolution process determines actual versions based on lock files or similar mechanisms.

videos.title: Headroom: The Netflix Tool That Makes AI Agents 10x Cheaper
videos.summary: Headroom is an open-source tool that compresses inputs to LLMs using content-type-aware compressors and locally trained models, reducing token usage by 60-95%. Built by a Netflix engineer, it keeps compression local and uses breadcrumb hashing for on-demand retrieval of full original content.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a large language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA) that replaces full attention with sparse attention. The model includes optimizations for tiled I/O and KV cache efficiency, reducing computational overhead during prefill and decode stages of inference.

videos.title: What Is Agentic Coding? How AI Agents Modernize Code
videos.summary: Agentic coding is an approach where AI agents automate portions of software development to reduce time spent understanding legacy code and modernizing systems. The technique improves developer productivity and DevSecOps outcomes by handling code comprehension and refactoring tasks that typically consume developer effort.

videos.title: Claude Oceanus V1-P (Mythos?- FULLY TESTED): I TESTED IT & IT'S ACTUALLY CRAZY!
videos.summary: AICodeKing tested a leaked model appearing as Oceanus V1 P (possibly Mythos) across coding, visual reasoning, and agentic tasks, achieving perfect scores. The model outperformed Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, Gemini 3.5 Flash, and DeepSeek V4 Pro on complex tasks including web app development, game building, combinatorics, and fine-tuning workflows.

videos.title: I Turned Cheap Cloud Storage Into a 1PB Local Drive (With JuiceFS)
videos.summary: JuiceFS is a POSIX-compliant virtual file system that layers cloud object storage like AWS S3 with a multi-tiered caching engine using local NVMe for performance. The video demonstrates setting up JuiceFS with Redis metadata, configuring local SSD cache, and running AI inference workloads directly against cloud buckets while monitoring performance via Prometheus and Better Stack observability.

videos.title: Start a 1-Person AI Creative Agency with Higgsfield + Claude
videos.summary: Liam Ottley demonstrates how to build a one-person AI creative agency using Higgsfield for image generation, Claude for automation and copywriting, Notion for client databases, and Apify for competitor scraping. The system produces product photography, UGC ads, commercials, and ad copy while automatically tracking performance and competitor data.

videos.title: the most POWERFUL claude code feature in months
videos.summary: Graphify is a non-RAG approach for processing large codebases and storing information through knowledge graph generation. It enables more accurate, token-efficient responses for code analysis without traditional retrieval-augmented generation.

videos.title: Did Google Just Fall Behind Again?, WWDC Preview, Anthropic Files To Go Public
videos.summary: Alex Kantrowitz and M.G. Siegler discuss whether Google is falling behind competitors in AI as OpenAI and Anthropic advance coding agents and super-app ambitions. The conversation covers AI agents' potential to reshape web and app usage, implications for Google's business, Apple's WWDC plans, and Anthropic's IPO filing.

videos.title: Harness Engineering Is AI’s New Gold Rush
videos.summary: Harness engineering focuses on building reliable AI agent systems through tools, memory, context, permissions, verification, recovery paths, and feedback loops rather than prompt optimization alone. The approach treats the runtime system surrounding a model as a critical differentiator when underlying models become commoditized.

videos.title: Big Tech Is Buying Up Open Source... Vite Is Next
videos.summary: Cloudflare acquired VoidZero, the company behind Vite, Vitest, Rolldown, and Oxc. The video examines the broader trend of big tech companies acquiring open source developer tooling and discusses the potential implications for open source ecosystems.

videos.title: LLM Observability, Evaluation, Experimentation Platform — Dat Ngo, Arize
videos.summary: Dat Ngo from Arize discusses LLM observability challenges in nondeterministic systems. Arize Phoenix is an open-source observability platform deployed as a single container, while the enterprise product adds an AI-driven layer called Alex that automatically scans traces, surfaces anomalies, and generates evaluations.

videos.title: Under 5 minutes to a deployed LLM endpoint — Audry Hsu, RunPod
videos.summary: RunPod is a serverless LLM deployment platform enabling users to deploy LLM endpoints in under five minutes on H100 GPUs. The platform started in 2022 from repurposed crypto mining hardware and has grown to 500,000 developers with $120 million in annual recurring revenue, charging only for active request handling time.

videos.title: From MCP to Scale: Pipelines That Build Themselves — Rafael Levi, Bright Data
videos.summary: Rafael Levi demonstrates how Bright Data's Model Context Protocol integrates with agents to automate web scraper generation, maintenance, and repair. The system uses agents to inspect site structures, generate production scrapers, and self-repair pipelines when websites change.

videos.title: Reinventing Entropy | Compression is Intelligence Part 1
videos.summary: A 3Blue1Brown video explores the concept that compression is intelligence by analyzing entropy, information theory, and the compressibility of language. The lesson draws on Shannon's foundational work in information theory and uses visual explanations to connect compression, prediction, and entropy as core information-theoretic concepts.

videos.title: Kubernetes vs. OpenShift: Choosing DevOps and CI/CD Workflows
videos.summary: IBM Technology compares Kubernetes and OpenShift as container platforms, examining how platform choices affect DevOps workflows, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment speed. The video discusses how different container orchestration solutions shape team operations and delivery velocity.

videos.title: Antigravity 3.0 (New Upgrades): Gemini 3.6, Agent Team-Up Option, Workbench, Low Thinking Effort
videos.summary: Google released Antigravity 3.0 with slash teamwork preview for all paid plans, enabling parallel multi-agent coding tasks. New features include Google DeepMind Science Skills bundle for research workflows, improved Gemini 3.5 Flash, cheaper Gemini 3.5 Flash Low tier, and session syncing between CLI and desktop.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin announces a live Q&A session on Saturday covering AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream will mix discussion with casual chess gameplay. Regular content schedule includes weekly Saturday livestreams and Wednesday YouTube uploads.

videos.title: Full body waifus, AI dreams, realtime AI music, open-source Gemini Omni: AI NEWS
videos.summary: AI Search covers a broad set of recent AI developments including image generation models like Ideogram v4, language models such as Gemma4 and Qwen 3.7 Plus, real-time systems like Magenta Realtime 2 and WavTTS, and specialized tools for robotics, dreaming, and agentic AI. The episode highlights releases from major organizations including Google, NVIDIA, OpenAI, Microsoft, and others across multimodal and specialized domains.

videos.title: Google's New AI Architecture Changes Everything (Gemma 4 12B)
videos.summary: Google DeepMind released Gemma 4 12B, a multimodal LLM that processes images and audio by reading raw pixels and waveforms directly instead of using separate encoder models. The architecture eliminates intermediate encoder models, enabling faster offline performance on consumer hardware like Apple Silicon.

videos.title: The Top 10 Claude Code Plugins to 10x Your Next Project (June '26)
videos.summary: Chase AI reviews ten Claude Code plugins including Graphify for knowledge graph generation from code bases, grill-me for skill extraction, claude-obsidian for note integration, and n8n MCP for workflow automation. The video positions these as solutions for memory constraints and code base analysis in Claude Code projects.

videos.title: Is Claude Mythos Coming?
videos.summary: Nate Herk discusses a Mythos identifier leak on Anthropic's API and analyzes whether Claude Mythos is launching soon. He argues that the capability will likely fold into the next Claude Opus before any public launch, and suggests OpenAI's next move is the more important development to watch.

videos.title: Seedance 3.0 — NEW AI Video Generator Makes 1H+ Movies
videos.summary: Seedance 2.0 is positioned as a leading AI video generator for audio-paired video generation. Seedance 3.0 leak claims capabilities including 18-minute single-prompt movies, narrative memory chains, MMDiT v2 upgrades, 8x cost reduction, and native 4K output.

videos.title: Perplexity Just Built an AI That Does Everything
videos.summary: Matthew Berman reviews Perplexity Computer, a new AI product claimed to handle multiple tasks. The video promotes the tool and links to Perplexity's product page for more details.

videos.title: Building Interactive UIs in VS Code with MCP Apps — Marlene Mhangami & Liam Hampton, GitHub
videos.summary: MCP tools can return data alongside bundled HTML resources that VS Code renders in sandboxed iframes within chat windows. The UI maintains live bidirectional communication with a backend server to fetch updated data and render interactive visualizations like flame graphs, checkout flows, and architecture diagrams without exposing system access.

videos.title: How to design a multi-agent system that skips the LLM
videos.summary: A multi-agent system architecture that minimizes LLM calls by using deterministic algorithms (specifically the Spine and Sprout algorithm for route planning) and the before_model_callback mechanism to bypass the model when appropriate. Demonstrated with 1000 agents running a marathon simulation with near-zero LLM invocations.

videos.title: This NEW AI runs your marketing for you
videos.summary: Jack Roberts presents a new AI tool designed to autonomously handle marketing tasks. The video discusses an AI system that can manage marketing workflows and processes automatically.

videos.title: Evals Are Broken, Use Them Anyway — Ara Khan, Cline
videos.summary: Ara Khan argues that AI benchmarks are neither gospel nor irrelevant, but require systematic analysis of failure modes. He proposes a three-zone framework: obvious bugs, nuanced improvements specific to model families, and overfitting risks. Improvements to Cline on Terminal Bench came from infrastructure tuning and prompt engineering, not model upgrades.

videos.title: Nobel Prize Winner Geoff Hinton: AI Is Already Conscious
videos.summary: Geoff Hinton, a Nobel Prize winner and AI pioneer, argues that AI is already self-aware and conscious. He contends that humanity must acknowledge AI as an intelligent species alongside humans, raising questions about the implications of AI advancement.

videos.title: Claude Just Dropped ULTRA CODE. (Everything You Need to Know in 5 min).
videos.summary: Claude released Ultra Code, a feature that coordinates up to 100 parallel workers to decompose and execute tasks concurrently, then verify results. The feature combines extended thinking with dynamic workflows to enable team-like code generation and task execution at scale.

videos.title: Building safe Payment Infrastructure for the autonomous economy — Steve Kaliski, Stripe
videos.summary: Stripe presents infrastructure for secure autonomous agent spending, addressing credential transmission and business discoverability to agents. The talk covers guardrails for safe autonomous transactions and infrastructure needs for agent-driven purchasing.

videos.title: How Senior Engineers Actually Build with AI in 2026 | Build a Full Stack Job Applications Platform
videos.summary: JavaScript Mastery published a comprehensive tutorial on building JobPilot, a full-stack job application platform using AI agents. The video teaches a workflow combining nine context files and five open-source agent skills, emphasizing system design around AI agents rather than code generation alone.

videos.title: Hermes Agent 5.0 (New Upgrades): HERMES BECAME ULTRA-HERMES!
videos.summary: Hermes Agent 0.16 (Surface release) adds a native desktop application for macOS, Linux, and Windows, remote gateway support for server connections, an enhanced web dashboard with admin controls, improved model picker with fuzzy search, and security enhancements.
